reason: shell-quote is a transitive dependency of 'concurrently', used only during local development (pnpm dev). It is never bundled into the Electron application or executed at runtime. The vulnerable code path (quote() with object .op tokens from untrusted input) is not reachable in this project because concurrently uses shell-quote internally for spawning fixed, developer-authored commands — no attacker-influenced input reaches quote(). Deployment model: local USB install on a controlled workstation with no external network attack surface.
remediationPath: Update concurrently when a patched version of shell-quote (>=1.8.4) is pulled in transitively.

reason: tmp is a transitive dependency via electron-builder > app-builder-lib > @malept/flatpak-bundler > tmp-promise > tmp. The flatpak bundler is not used in this deployment workflow (Windows/macOS/Linux USB distribution only — no Flatpak targets). The vulnerable path-traversal surface requires attacker-controlled prefix/postfix/dir options; in this build toolchain those values are hardcoded constants, not user-supplied input. Build tooling runs in a controlled local/CI environment only.
remediationPath: Monitor electron-builder for an upstream fix that updates the tmp transitive dependency to >=0.2.6.

reason: esbuild is a transitive dependency of vite (build tooling only, never in the Electron runtime bundle). The vulnerability affects the esbuild Deno module (lib/deno/mod.ts) which downloads native binaries without SHA-256 verification when NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY is attacker-controlled. This project does not use the Deno distribution of esbuild — vite uses the Node.js npm package, which has integrity verification. The vulnerable Deno code path is not executed. Upgrading esbuild directly would conflict with vite's peer dependency constraints; the fix requires a vite release that vendors a patched esbuild.
remediationPath: Upgrade vite to a version that requires esbuild >=0.28.1 when available without breaking Node 20.11.1 compatibility.

reason: form-data is a transitive dependency via electron-builder > app-builder-lib > electron-publish > form-data, used only by electron-builder's release-publishing tooling at build time. It is never bundled into the shipped Electron application or executed at runtime. The vulnerable CRLF injection surface requires attacker-controlled multipart field names/filenames reaching form-data's encoder; in this build toolchain the only caller is electron-publish's internal upload logic operating on fixed, developer-controlled artifact paths — no attacker-influenced input reaches the vulnerable code path. Deployment model: local USB install on a controlled workstation with no external network attack surface.
remediationPath: Monitor electron-builder for an update that pulls in form-data >=4.0.6.

reason: undici is a transitive dependency via electron-builder > app-builder-lib > @electron/rebuild > node-gyp > undici, used only during native-module rebuild steps in the local/CI build toolchain. It is never bundled into the shipped Electron application or executed at runtime. The vulnerable WebSocket fragment-count DoS requires a malicious/compromised WebSocket peer; node-gyp's use of undici is for outbound HTTP(S) downloads of prebuilt binaries, not for accepting inbound WebSocket connections, so the vulnerable code path is not exercised. Deployment model: local USB install on a controlled workstation with no external network attack surface.
remediationPath: Monitor electron-builder for an update that pulls in node-gyp/undici >=6.27.0.

reason: ws is a transitive dependency of jsdom, a devDependency used only to provide a DOM environment for Vitest unit tests. It is never bundled into the shipped Electron application or executed at runtime. The vulnerable memory-exhaustion DoS requires a malicious WebSocket peer sending crafted tiny fragments; jsdom's use of ws (if any is even reachable) is confined to the local test process and never accepts external network connections in this project's test suite. Deployment model: local USB install on a controlled workstation with no external network attack surface.
remediationPath: Monitor jsdom for an update that pulls in ws >=8.21.0.
